title: 5 Weekday Dinners — June 15–19, 2026
description: Built around the current fridge/freezer inventory and CSA vegetables, with every dinner including a protein, carb, and vegetable. Portions are sized for two adults plus Aria, with leftovers or adult lunch padding where useful.
useFirst: Delicate greens, cilantro, butter/green/purple lettuces, older broccoli/chopped kale, and any already-thawed seafood.
useLater: Lacinato kale, beet roots, green garlic/leeks, frozen proteins, and shelf-stable grains.
timeTarget: All recipes are designed for under 60 minutes; most are 30–45 minutes with rice/quinoa started first.
buyIfNeeded: Ginger, extra lemons/limes, one cucumber, avocado or fruit for toddler sides, and fresh fish only if the thawed mahi is no longer in its safe window.

notes.title: Food-safety note on the thawed mahi mahi
notes.body: USDA guidance says seafood thawed in the refrigerator should generally be cooked within another 1–2 days. If the mahi mahi was already thawed on Friday, do not hold it for Monday dinner; use it Sunday or replace with fresh/frozen fish.

notes.title: Optional prep that makes the week easier
notes.body: Cook extra sushi rice on Tuesday or Wednesday for Wednesday/Thursday/Friday; wash and dry lettuces; cut beet/turnip greens from roots; thaw pork belly overnight Thursday for Friday.

days.title: Oven lemon-garlic mahi mahi, pearl couscous, and sautéed spinach/green garlic
ingredients: 1.25–1.5 lb mahi mahi fillets, patted dry
2¼ cups low-sodium chicken broth, vegetable broth, or water
6–8 cups CSA spinach or tender greens, washed and chopped
2 green garlic stalks or garlic scapes, thinly sliced; separate firmer white/light-green parts from tender green tops if applicable
1 lemon, zested and juiced; optional extra lemon wedges for serving
½ tsp paprika or smoked paprika
½ tsp dried oregano or thyme
Salt and pepper; keep Aria’s portion lighter on salt
steps: Heat oven to 425°F. Line a sheet pan with parchment or foil for easier cleanup.
Start couscous: toast it in 1 tbsp olive oil for 2 minutes, add broth/water, cover, and simmer 10–12 minutes until tender. Rest covered.
While couscous cooks, mix lemon zest, half the lemon juice, garlic, paprika, oregano/thyme, 1 tbsp olive oil, pepper, and a moderate pinch of salt. Rub over the mahi mahi.

Put mahi mahi on the sheet pan. Scatter the firmer white/light-green green garlic or scape pieces around the fish with a tiny drizzle of olive oil. Bake 8–12 minutes, depending on thickness, until the fish flakes easily and reaches 145°F.
While the fish bakes, sauté the tender green garlic tops and spinach in a large skillet with a little olive oil and a splash of water, 3–5 minutes, until wilted. Finish with remaining lemon juice.
Fluff couscous. Serve mahi mahi over couscous with sautéed spinach/green garlic alongside; spoon any lemony pan juices over the adult plates.
days.ariaServing: flake fish carefully and check for bones; serve with couscous and finely chopped cooked spinach/green garlic, keeping her portion lower salt.
days.note: Use the mahi mahi only if it is still inside a safe thawed-seafood window; otherwise swap in fresh or frozen white fish and cook to doneness.

days.title: Skin-on sockeye salmon, quinoa, and roasted broccoli–kale
ingredients: 1 large head broccoli or 12–16 oz florets
1 lemon, half sliced and half juiced
Salt, pepper, and optional garlic powder
steps: Heat oven to 425°F. Start quinoa with water/broth; simmer covered about 15 minutes, then rest 5 minutes.
Toss broccoli with olive oil, salt, pepper, and garlic. Roast on a sheet pan 12 minutes.
Add chopped kale to the sheet pan with a small drizzle of oil. Toss and push vegetables to one side.
Pat salmon dry. Season with pepper, a little salt, lemon juice, and optional garlic powder. Add to the sheet pan skin-side down with lemon slices on top.
Roast 8–12 minutes, depending on thickness, until salmon flakes and reaches your preferred doneness.
Serve salmon with quinoa and broccoli–kale; squeeze lemon over the adult plates.
days.ariaServing: check carefully for bones, remove skin if tough, and flake salmon into small pieces.

days.title: Soy–sesame ground pork lettuce rice bowls
ingredients: 1–2 heads CSA butter/green/purple lettuce, washed and dried
1 tbsp grated ginger, or ½ tsp ground ginger
1 tbsp rice vinegar or lemon/lime juice
Optional adults-only: chili crisp, sriracha, or gochujang at the table
steps: Start sushi rice in the rice cooker or on the stove.
Brown pork in a large skillet over medium-high heat, breaking it up well, 6–8 minutes. Drain excess fat if needed.
Add garlic, ginger, and the white/light-green scallion parts. Cook 1 minute.
Stir in soy sauce, vinegar/lemon, and sesame oil. Simmer 1–2 minutes until glossy.
Chop some lettuce for bowls and leave some leaves whole for wraps. Toss lettuce with cilantro, remaining scallions, a squeeze of lemon/lime, and a tiny drizzle of oil.
Serve rice topped with pork and lettuce salad; adults can add chili at the table.
days.ariaServing: rice + pork with very finely chopped lettuce or a cooked green on the side; skip chili and go light on salty sauce.

days.title: Shrimp fried rice with spinach, scallions, and eggs
days.time: 25–35 min with cooked rice; under 60 with fresh rice
ingredients: 1 lb raw shrimp, peeled/deveined if needed
4–5 cups cooked sushi rice, ideally chilled or at least cooled
4 cups spinach or mixed delicate greens, chopped
Optional: frozen peas/carrots if you have them
steps: If rice is not already cooked, start it first and spread it on a tray for a few minutes so it steams off.
Pat shrimp dry. Heat oil in a wok or large skillet; cook shrimp 1–2 minutes per side until pink. Remove and chop a few pieces smaller for Aria.
Add a little more oil if needed. Scramble eggs quickly, then move them to the shrimp plate.
Cook garlic and scallion whites 30 seconds. Add rice and stir-fry 3–5 minutes, breaking up clumps.
Add spinach/greens and cook until wilted. Return shrimp and eggs. Season with soy sauce and sesame oil.
Finish with scallion greens and cilantro.
days.ariaServing: cut shrimp into small pieces lengthwise/crosswise, and keep her portion lower sodium.

days.title: Pork belly lettuce wraps with rice and sangchu-geotjeori-style salad
days.note: For the lettuce salad baseline, use the household standard: Maangchi’s sangchu-geotjeori — https://www.maangchi.com/recipe/sangchu-geotjeori. The version below keeps Aria’s portion unspiced/less salty and lets adults add heat separately.
ingredients: 1–1.25 lb frozen pork belly slices, thawed overnight
2 heads or several big handfuls CSA lettuce leaves, washed and dried
½ cup cilantro, optional but useful if still fresh
1 cucumber, optional buy, cut into sticks
Adults-only add-ons: gochujang, ssamjang, kimchi, chili crisp, or gochugaru
steps: Start sushi rice. While it cooks, wash and dry lettuce thoroughly.
For a mild family lettuce salad, toss chopped lettuce with scallions, cilantro, lemon/rice vinegar, soy sauce, sesame oil, and sesame seeds. Pull out Aria’s portion before adding adult heat.
Heat a cast-iron pan or griddle over medium-high. Cook pork belly slices in batches until browned and cooked through, 2–4 minutes per side depending on thickness.
Drain pork on a towel-lined plate. Cut into bite-size pieces.
Serve as wraps: lettuce leaf + rice + pork + cucumber + adult sauce. Or serve deconstructed bowls for easier toddler eating.
days.ariaServing: choose leaner, softer pork pieces, cut very small; serve rice and mild lettuce/cucumber separately.

groceryList: Only if not stocked: ginger, extra lemons/limes, cucumber, low-sodium broth, sesame seeds.
Optional toddler sides: avocado, berries/fruit, plain yogurt, or cottage cheese.
Fresh/frozen white fish only if the already-thawed mahi mahi is not used Sunday and needs replacing.
